title: Baroque Dining Room Composer
description: The user designs a baroque dining room by placing ornate purple-gold damask chairs around a table and arranging decorative avocado centerpieces on it. A material budget system conserves total gold-leaf units across all placed objects, requiring strategic allocation between chair gilding and avocado pedestals, with full undo/redo support.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a baroque dining room composition tool. Load ChairDamaskPurplegold.glb and Avocado.glb from shared_assets/. Scene setup: a rectangular wooden dining table (procedural box with wood-tone material) centered in a room with a checkered marble floor and warm ambient plus directional lighting. Camera uses OrbitControls. Core mechanics: The user has a toolbar with two buttons (Add Chair, Add Avocado) and a gold-leaf budget of 100 units displayed as a top HUD bar. Clicking Add Chair places a clone of the damask chair at a random open position around the table perimeter, costing 15 gold units. Clicking Add Avocado places a scaled-up avocado (scale 30) on the table surface as a centerpiece, costing 8 gold units. Each placed object is selectable by raycasting on click. When selected, an object highlights with an emissive outline and a side panel appears showing: a rotation slider (0-360 degrees Y-axis), a scale slider (0.8x-1.5x), and a gold-leaf allocation slider that lets the user redistribute 0-10 bonus gold units to that object, increasing its emissive gold tint proportionally. Bonus gold is deducted from the shared budget, enforcing conservation: increasing one object's allocation decreases available budget, preventing overspend. Multi-input coupling: holding Shift while dragging an object snaps it to a grid and simultaneously adjusts neighboring objects' rotation to face the dragged object. Implement an undo/redo stack (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z) that tracks all actions: placements, deletions (Delete key removes selected object and refunds its gold cost), slider changes, and position moves. Display an action history count. The user must place at least 4 chairs and 2 avocados, adjust gold allocation on 3 objects, delete and re-place one item, and use undo/redo at least once to complete the room, totaling 10-14 interaction steps. Show a completion checklist overlay tracking these goals.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  goldBudget: number,         // remaining gold-leaf units available for allocation
  totalChairs: number,         // count of damask chairs placed in the scene
  totalAvocados: number,         // count of avocado centerpieces placed on the table
  selectedObjectId: string,         // unique identifier of the currently selected object or empty string
  selectedObjectRotation: number,         // Y-axis rotation in degrees of the selected object
  selectedObjectScale: number,         // uniform scale multiplier of the selected object
  selectedObjectGoldAllocation: number,         // bonus gold-leaf units allocated to the selected object
  totalGoldAllocated: number,         // sum of all bonus gold allocated across all objects
  isObjectSelected: boolean,         // whether any object is currently selected
  canAffordChair: boolean,         // whether the budget allows placing another chair
}
